We share the motor industry association's concerns that the current CVS targets significantly outpace consumer demand and risk undermining new vehicle affordability and vehicle choice.

And we recommend that the Clean Vehicle standard bill is scrapped for the following reasons. The CVS will penalize many critical industries for contributing so much to New Zealand's economy, such as our farming, building and construction, forestry and energy sectors.
These industries require vehicles that can operate safely and effectively in New Zealand's toughest terrain. This bill will inflate the price of those vehicles between 2000 and $8,000.

The CVS will create an inflationary cycle. Not only does it increase the cost of new vehicles to businesses who may pass those costs to their customers inflation that also inflates the costs of those vehicles to those who enjoy their lifestyles connection to the outdoors sport.

DIY New Zealanders travel distances we to heavy loads and have high levels of personal use for tools of trade vehicles. These charges represent a new form of tax on businesses and private consumers and may impact public confidence whilst they feel p alive purchasing a new vehicle.

Even now with the Clean Vehicle Standard charges, we feel that they should appear as a cost line on all new vehicle invoices for transparency reasons to the consumer. We also seek transparency on how the fund is being utilized. As consumer costs increase their potential impacts, households and businesses may hold onto the current vehicles for longer.

Newer vehicles have emission reductions that will reduce the effectiveness of the bill with less new vehicles purchased. Automotive dealerships may feel the pressures from decreased, decreased revenue, which may lead to increased unemployment across our industry or business closures.

The technology to require to ca to cater for those productive views or cases that we discussed is not yet available, nor is it expected to be affordable under the current CVS bill. Those hybrid vehicles will now also incur a fee of $800 to $2,000.

In conclusion, we would urge the committee to adopt a more pragmatic evidence-based approach that supports a mission reduction whilst protecting consumer needs, affordability and industry viability. The clean vehicle standard is unworkable, unfair, and should be scrapped.

And uh, one of the purposes of the Clean Car standard was to promote the technologies that help bring cleaner vehicles to New Zealand. Um, used imports have fallen 20 to 25% over the last year or so. Uh, and that was from a, uh, almost similar fall the year before.

We've gone from averaging volumes of around 120,000 vehicles a year to expecting somewhere around 80,000 to 90,000 vehicles, if not fewer. Uh, what this means is that we are not turning over the fleet while we are bringing in vehicles that are nine to 10 years old.

They are replacing vehicles that can be 20 years old. Uh, so we are the midlife replacement midlife refresh. Uh, we, we provide a different service than the new car industry, the new car industry.
